draft.introduction: A psalm of confident trust combining petition for divine protection with longing for the Temple. The phrase “seek his face” became a touchstone for mystical theology.

translation.text: The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? The Lord is the stronghold of my life; of whom shall I be afraid? One thing have I asked of the Lord: that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, to gaze upon the beauty of the Lord.
translation.original_text: κύριος φωτισμός μου καὶ σωτήρ μου τίνα φοβηθήσομαι

translator_notes.note: The Greek τερπνότητα (“beauty, delight”) translates Hebrew no‘am. Gregory of Nyssa used this verse to argue that the soul’s desire for God grows eternally—his doctrine of epektasis.
